ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE MEDIATION SET FOR OCT-13-2004 AT 10:00 AM IN DEPT. 212. NOTICE SENT BY COURT

DISCOVERY 610, MOTION ARGUED. AFTER ARGUMENT, TENTATIVE ADOPTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. NOTWITHSTANDING DECISIONAL LAW INTERPRETING THE RIGHT OF PRIVACY AS PROHIBITING THE RELEASE OF ARREST RECORDS OR DISSEMINATION OF INFORMATION ABOUT ARRESTS, BECAUSE THERE WAS NO TIMELY OBJECTION TO THE QUESTION OF WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAD PREVIOUSLY BEEN ARRESTED, ANY OBJECTIONS TO THE SPECIFIC QUESTION ARE WAIVED. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE QUESTIONS RELATING TO TATTOOS AND WHETHER THE PLAINTIFF EVER BELONGED TO A GANG. THE COURT SUSTAINS THE OBJECTIONS BASED ON THE RIGHT TO PRIVACY. IF ORAL ARGUMENT IS REQUESTED, APPEAR AT 9:30 AM ON SEPTEMBER 7, 2004. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
